description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a functional, industrial appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a dark coloration that contrasts with the lighter, textured top. The block appears dense and heavy, likely made from a durable stone such as granite or concrete. Its design suggests it could serve as a drainage cover, a tactile paving element, or a barrier in an urban environment. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering on its surfaces.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, dark sides. The block has a functional, industrial look suitable for urban infrastructure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, grooved top surface with horizontal stripes and solid, dark front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and regularity of the grooves, with the stripes running vertically from this angle.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved top and solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or street drainage area
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: set flush with adjacent pavement or stonework
